nodes.name: InitializeSampler
nodes.description: Initializes the NUTS sampler state, including the random seed, initial positions, and target acceptance probability.

inputs.name: target
inputs.constraints: A function that computes the log-probability (and its gradient) of the target distribution.

inputs.name: initial_positions
inputs.constraints: The starting point(s) for the MCMC chain(s).

inputs.name: target_accept_p
inputs.constraints: The target acceptance probability for adapting the step size, typically between 0 and 1.

inputs.name: seed
inputs.constraints: Seed for the pseudo-random number generator.

outputs.name: initial_sampler_state
outputs.constraints: An immutable object containing the initial position, PRNG key, and other sampler parameters.

nodes.type_signature: (target: Callable, initial_positions: ArrayLike, target_accept_p: float, seed: int) -> SamplerState
nodes.conceptual_summary: InitializeStochasticProcessState. Packages an initial state vector, a potential function defining a high-dimensional landscape, a seed for a pseudo-random number generator, and adaptive control parameters into a single, self-contained initial state object. This object serves as the t=0 configuration for an iterative, stochastic process designed to traverse the landscape.. Applications: Initializing a molecular dynamics simulation with starting atomic coordinates, a force-field potential, and a random seed for the thermostat., Seeding the initial population and fitness function for an evolutionary algorithm searching a complex solution space., Configuring the initial state estimate and system model for a Kalman filter in autonomous vehicle navigation., Setting the starting parameters and random seed for training a generative adversarial network (GAN).

nodes.name: RunSampler
nodes.description: Runs the NUTS MCMC sampler by repeatedly applying its transition kernel to generate a chain of samples, after discarding a specified number of burn-in steps.

inputs.name: initial_sampler_state
inputs.constraints: The initial state from which to start the sampling process.

inputs.name: n_collect
inputs.constraints: The number of samples to collect and return.

inputs.name: n_discard
inputs.constraints: The number of initial (burn-in) samples to discard.

outputs.name: collected_samples
outputs.constraints: The collected samples from the posterior distribution.

outputs.name: final_sampler_state
outputs.constraints: The final state of the sampler after the run.

nodes.type_signature: (initial_sampler_state: SamplerState, n_collect: int, n_discard: int) -> tuple[ArrayLike, SamplerState]
nodes.conceptual_summary: IterativeStateSpaceExplorer. Generates a sequence of points in a state space by repeatedly applying a stochastic transition function, starting from an initial configuration. An initial portion of the generated sequence is discarded to allow the process to converge to a stable regime, after which a specified number of subsequent points are collected. This effectively samples points from the stationary distribution of the underlying Markov chain.. Applications: Simulating particle configurations in statistical mechanics to compute macroscopic material properties (e.g., pressure, energy)., Exploring the solution space of complex combinatorial optimization problems, such as logistics routing or circuit design, via methods like Simulated Annealing., Generating plausible trajectories and configuration spaces for robotic path planning in uncertain or dynamic environments., Forecasting the evolution of complex systems in econometrics or epidemiology where future states are probabilistic functions of the present state.

nodes.name: create_progress_channels
nodes.description: Initializes a set of sender/receiver pairs (channels) for inter-thread communication, one for each MCMC chain, to report progress statistics.

inputs.name: n_chains
inputs.constraints: Number of chains to create channels for.

outputs.name: senders
outputs.constraints: Vector of channel senders for worker threads.

outputs.name: receivers
outputs.constraints: Vector of channel receivers for the monitoring thread.

nodes.name: launch_progress_monitor
nodes.description: Spawns a background thread to aggregate progress statistics from all chains and display them using a multi-progress bar. It calculates and shows live metrics like acceptance probability and R-hat.

inputs.name: receivers
inputs.constraints: Channel receivers to listen for statistics from.

inputs.name: n_collect
inputs.constraints: Number of samples to be collected.

inputs.name: n_discard
inputs.constraints: Number of samples to be discarded (burn-in).

outputs.name: progress_handle
outputs.constraints: A handle to the spawned progress monitoring thread.

nodes.type_signature: (receivers: Vec<Receiver<ChainStats>>, n_collect: int, n_discard: int) -> ThreadHandle

nodes.name: run_chains_in_parallel
nodes.description: Executes multiple MCMC chains concurrently in separate threads. Each chain runs a sampler for a specified number of steps and sends progress updates through its designated channel.

inputs.name: chains
inputs.constraints: The MCMC chain objects to be run.

inputs.name: senders
inputs.constraints: Channels for each chain to report progress.

inputs.name: n_collect
inputs.constraints: Number of samples to collect per chain.

inputs.name: n_discard
inputs.constraints: Number of samples to discard per chain.

outputs.name: chain_samples
outputs.constraints: A vector containing the 2D sample tensor from each completed chain.

nodes.type_signature: (chains: Vec<Chain>, senders: Vec<Sender<ChainStats>>, n_collect: int, n_discard: int) -> Vec<Tensor>

nodes.name: stack_chain_samples
nodes.description: Merges the list of 2D sample tensors from individual chains into a single 3D tensor, where the first dimension represents the chain index.

inputs.name: chain_samples
inputs.constraints: A list of 2D tensors, one for each chain.

outputs.name: stacked_samples
outputs.constraints: A single 3D tensor containing all samples from all chains.

nodes.name: calculate_run_statistics
nodes.description: Computes summary statistics, such as the R-hat convergence diagnostic, from the final stacked sample tensor across all chains.

inputs.name: stacked_samples
inputs.constraints: A 3D tensor of MCMC samples (chains, draws, dims).

nodes.name: finalize_run
nodes.description: Waits for the progress monitoring thread to complete its execution and packages the final samples and run statistics into a single result object.

inputs.name: stacked_samples
inputs.constraints: The final 3D tensor of all MCMC samples.

inputs.name: run_stats
inputs.constraints: The summary statistics for the run.

inputs.name: progress_handle
inputs.constraints: The handle for the monitoring thread to join.

outputs.name: final_samples
outputs.constraints: The final 3D tensor of all MCMC samples.

outputs.name: final_stats
outputs.constraints: The summary statistics for the run.

nodes.type_signature: (stacked_samples: Tensor, run_stats: RunStats, progress_handle: ThreadHandle) -> tuple[Tensor, RunStats]
